item.title: New Fender James Jamerson 1962 Precision Bass 3997gr — $3,457.40
item.description:
$3,457.40 · Brand New · Alteisa

DescriptionThe James Jamerson 1962 Precision Bass® – your gateway to the legendary sound that powered the Motown revolution.

James Jamerson is widely recognized as the single most influential bassist of all time. Consistently ranked #1 on “greatest bassists” lists, he laid down the groove on dozens of hit songs that defined the Motown sound. His incredible musical prowess and signature style didn’t just define a genre – Jamerson rewrote the rulebook for what was possible on electric bass, influencing legends like Paul McCartney, Marcus Miller, John Entwistle, and Geddy Lee.

The Bass That Built Motown

Jamerson’s signature instrument was his beloved 1962 Precision Bass – the foundation beneath countless hits that shaped popular music forever. This stunning eracorrect tribute instrument celebrates James’ legacy and his inseparable association with the Precision Bass, delivering the authentic vintage sound and feel that defined an era.

Authentic Vintage Craftsmanship

Every detail honors the original, from the premium Heirloom Nitrocellulose Lacquer finish to the era-correct neck shape that captures the feel of Jamerson’s legendary instrument. The custom James Jamerson ‘62 Precision Bass pickup celebrates those unmistakable tones that anchored the Motown hit factory.

Premium Heritage Features

The Heirloom™ Nitrocellulose™ lacquer finish delivers the authentic spirit and aging characteristics that made the original so special. The premium tone and meticulous attention to detail ensure this bass performs to the highest professional standards. Experience the James Jamerson Precision Bass and feel the difference that inspired generations.
Features

  • Alder Body with Heirloom™ Nitrocellulose Lacquer Finish
  • James Jamerson 1962 "C"-shaped Maple neck with Heirloom™ Nitrocellulose Lacquer finish and 7.25" radius rosewood fingerboard
  • Custom-voiced James Jamerson '62 Precision Bass Pickup
  • Vintage-Style Hardware including Pickup and Bridge Covers
  • La Bella 0760M Stainless Steel Flatwound Strings
  • Custom Certificate of Authenticity and Commemorative Booklet

item.description:
$2,101.20 · Brand New · KUROSAWA MUSIC G-CLUB UMEDA

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――

MOMOSE Custom Craft Guitars is a brand of traditional electric guitars and basses named after “Yasuo Momose,” a senior master builder at the Asuka Factory in Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture.

It is no exaggeration to say that this brand—which has enjoyed enduring popularity for so long that it has become synonymous with high-quality Japanese-made guitars—is meticulously crafted one instrument at a time by skilled artisans. From wood selection to processing and finishing, the brand consistently pursues the “ideal musical instrument.”


While preserving the fundamental structure unique to vintage instruments, the brand incorporates meticulous attention to detail throughout and places a strong emphasis on playability, achieving a level of quality that professionals can rely on in their work.

This high level of craftsmanship offers exceptional value for money within the industry and has garnered widespread support from both professionals and amateurs alike.


The model we are introducing today is an original design that inherits the traditional style of the past while incorporating Momose’s unique refinements.


It retains the hallmarks that have defined Momose since its founding: meticulous, handcrafted processing by skilled artisans who discern the unique character of each piece of wood; fingerboard binding made from the same wood as the fingerboard; a heel cut with a recessed joint plate; and a thin top lacquer finish.

With refined specifications down to the finest details, this is truly a model that represents the company’s utmost dedication.



This model features an ash body paired with a rosewood fingerboard.

The recessed joint plate at the neck joint ensures excellent playability even in the higher frets while producing a traditional sound.


As with previous models, the fingerboard sides feature wood binding, and as a new specification, it incorporates Luminray side dots—a phosphorescent material that proves invaluable in studios and on stage.


The tuners are the compact and stylish GOTOH® GBU510C-12.

GOTOH®’s high-precision craftsmanship ensures stable tuning.


The guitar is equipped with the “Gravitas,” a Momose original pickup newly developed for the 2026 redesign.

This original pickup optimizes the balance of sound across each string; with a lower center of gravity than previous models, it delivers a sound with greater body and density.

While rich in body, it retains the characteristic contour of Momose’s sound, resulting in a tone that captures the essence of passive pickups and maintains a distinct presence that won’t get lost in an ensemble.


Additionally, pulling up the tone knob activates the “Turbo Switch,” which connects the two pickups in series.

Despite being a passive circuit, this expands the tonal range and enhances versatility.

In conjunction with the development of the new pickups, the capacitors were also redesigned, and Centralab oil capacitors were adopted. These provide excellent responsiveness to the player’s nuances and deliver a brightness that makes the sound project forward.


The jacks feature Puretone® jacks, renowned for their low noise. Designed with meticulous attention to detail, they are built to resist loosening even after long-term use, ensuring stable performance to support the player’s musical endeavors.


With upgrades throughout, this instrument stands out as a masterpiece offering even greater value for the price.


It’s a ready-to-play guitar packed with the meticulous attention to detail that Momose is known for.


We invite you to try it out.


【SPEC】

Body：Ash 2P
Neck：Maple
Fingerboard：Maple
Frets：Jescar FW55090NS (9662NS)
Nut：Oiled Bone
Machineheads：GOTOH GBU-510C-12
Bridge：GOTOH 404BO-5
Pickups：Gravitas-J-Five set
Controls：2Vol,1Tone(Pull up: Turbo SW)
Scale：864mm(34inch)
Width at Nut：46.0mm
Neck Grip：C Shape
Radius：400R
Finish：Top Lacquer

item.title: Ibanez Mode MDM1005-PW Pearl White - 5-String Electric Bass — $1,204.65
item.description:
$1,204.65 · Brand New · MUSIC STORE professional GmbH

  • 'Mode' body made from alder
  • Roasted Maple Neck with MDM5 neck profile
  • Ibanez Super All-Access neck joint
  • Rosewood fingerboard with 24 medium stainless steel frets
  • 12" fretboard radius (305 mm)
  • White dot inlays and luminescent side dots
  • Two Ibanez Delta S5J pickups
  • Ibanez Sonitech S preamp with 3-band tone control
  • Controls for volume, tone (passive), balance, bass, mid and treble
  • EQ bypass switch and 3-way mid-frequency switch
  • Ibanez MR5S mono-rail bridge
  • Ibanez Aero-light tuners
  • Pearl White high-gloss finish
  • Includes a gig bag
The Ibanez Mode MDM1005-PW Pearl White at a glance

The Ibanez MDM1005-PW Pearl White is a professional 5-string electric bass that combines state-of-the-art technology with an exceptionally versatile range of sounds. This is made possible by the powerful Ibanez Sonitech S active electronics, which, together with the two Ibanez Delta S5J pickups, enable a wide spectrum of diverse bass sounds. In active mode, a precise 3-band equaliser with a three-position switchable mid-frequency control is available to optimally adapt the sound to any playing style and musical situation. Thanks to the integrated EQ bypass switch, the bass can also be played in passive mode, with a classic tone control regulating the treble. High-quality Ibanez Aero-light tuners ensure reliable tuning stability, whilst the Ibanez MR5S single-rail bridge guarantees precise intonation and a direct response. The professional features of the Ibanez MDM1600-NML are rounded off by a custom-fit gig bag, which is included as standard.

item.title: Hofner 500/1 Violin / Beatle 4-string Hollowbody Bass with Case — $2,749
item.description:
$2,749 · Excellent · Atomic Music

This is a pre-owned/used instrument available as shown for local delivery, pickup at our storefront (curbside or in store) in Beltsville, Maryland, or shipment to those in the lower 48/USA only via UPS Ground [a signature will be required for delivery]*.

  • Its electronics have been tested and function 100% as they should.
  • See the provided pix for best idea of overall cosmetic condition. As it is pre-owned, it has acquired surface marks (scratches, scuffs, smudges, etc.) from play/use - all purely cosmetic in nature. There are no issues to note with regard to functionality and playability.
  • Its original case will be included, as shown.  No other extras are available for this item.
  • This listing has been created for the benefit of our local customers.  There may be cosmetic or functional issues that the pictures alone cannot capture — if you are not able to come by and inspect this instrument in person, please be sure to ask any relevant questions you may have regarding its condition, playability, etc. before buying.

* Please be aware that humidity, temperature, and handling can affect guitars & basses such that adjustments to the instrument might be needed post-shipment. This includes "fret sprout" (as wood can shrink/swell during shipment, a fretted instrument may arrive with sharp fret edges). If tweaking action, intonation, etc., having fret edges dressed, or having the instrument set up to your specs is not something you are comfortable with doing yourself, you will need the help of a local-to-you tech/professional. Note that any associated fees incurred for their time and labor will be your responsibility.

Please contact us to discuss any other questions you may have about it before clicking the "Add To Cart" button!


Atomic Music store hours:  12pm - 6pm, EST, Monday - Saturday & 12pm - 5pm, EST, Sundays.

Thanks!!

- Atomic Music

item.title: 1978 Fender Musicmaster Bass – Dakota Red – Schaller Bridge – Used Very Good — $1,299
item.description:
$1,299 · Very Good · Mill River Music & Guitar Shop

1978 Fender Musicmaster Bass – Dakota Red – Schaller Bridge – Used Very Good
The Musicmaster Bass occupies a genuinely charming corner of Fender history. Introduced in 1971 as Fender's most affordable bass offering, it was built largely from leftover Mustang and guitar parts — an alder body in the familiar offset Mustang shape, a short 30-inch scale maple neck with rosewood fingerboard, and most famously, a six-pole Stratocaster guitar pickup hiding under a solid plastic cover in the middle position. What sounds like a compromise in design terms turns out to be part of the instrument's character — the guitar pickup gives the Musicmaster a punchy, bright, slightly midrange-forward voice that sits somewhere between a traditional bass tone and something more percussive and cutting. It is not a deep, tubby short-scale bass. It is fast, articulate, and fun, and it has attracted a devoted cult following among players who have discovered what it can do. The 30-inch scale and slim neck make it genuinely comfortable for players with smaller hands, for long sessions, or for guitarists doubling on bass, and the simple one volume, one tone control layout keeps things elegantly uncomplicated. This 1978 example comes from a well-regarded period of Musicmaster Bass production — USA-made in Fullerton, California, with the construction quality and finish work that vintage Fender collectors look for.
The bridge has been upgraded to a Schaller unit — a practical and sonically beneficial swap that addresses one of the few weak points of the original design. The footprint of the original bridge is visible on the body where the finish protected the surrounding area, shown clearly in the photos. Otherwise this bass appears to be all original, including the pickup, electronics, and tuners. The rosewood fingerboard is beautiful, no fret wear, just set up through our shop and playing great. The Dakota Red finish is in really nice shape with some gentle checking consistent with the instrument's age — a wonderful patina on a wonderful color. And yes, there is a dragon sticker on the front that, we have to say, suits this bass remarkably well. This one has quickly become a shop favorite — the kind of bass that is genuinely hard to put down once you pick it up.
- Make: Fender
- Model: Musicmaster Bass
- Year: 1978 (serial number S837481)
- Country of origin: USA (Fullerton, CA)
- Body: Alder, offset Mustang shape
- Finish: Dakota Red, gloss
- Neck: Maple, bolt-on
- Fingerboard: Rosewood, dot inlays
- Scale length: 30"
- Nut width: 1-1/2"
- Frets: 19, no fret wear
- Pickup: Original Fender 6-pole Stratocaster-style single coil under solid plastic cover
- Controls: Master volume, master tone
- Bridge: Schaller (upgraded, original bridge marks visible on body)
- Tuners: Original Fender
- Hardware: Chrome
- Modifications: Schaller bridge installed
- Condition: Used Very Good – gentle finish checking consistent with age, original bridge marks visible on body, beautiful rosewood board, plays great
- Includes: None
Ships carefully packed and insured for full value. International buyers are welcome — please contact us for a shipping quote before purchasing.
About Mill River Music
Mill River Music is an independent, human-powered music shop located in downtown Northampton, MA, run by a team of players, enthusiasts, and certified repair technicians. Every piece of used gear we sell is personally inspected, tested, and set up before it ships — we don't list it until we'd be happy to play it ourselves.
As Martin and Taylor certified techs, we hold our guitar work to a high standard, and we're committed to full transparency about the condition of everything we sell. No surprises.
When you buy from Mill River, you're supporting an independent local business that genuinely cares — about gear, about players, and about doing things right. We're here every step of the way. Reach out anytime.
Anything feel off about that listing, or any questions you wish I had asked? I'll note it for our next instructions review.

The “MJ” is part of the original series from the popular Japanese brand “Momose.”

This model combines traditional specifications with modern playability and sound.


The model featured here is the “MJ1-STD/R,” a jazz bass featuring an alder body, a maple neck, and a rosewood fingerboard.

The body-to-neck joint is slanted, and the joint plate is recessed to improve playability in the higher positions. The instrument is packed with craftsmanship, such as wood binding along the sides of the fingerboard to conceal the fret posts.

It is equipped with the original VJ-1 pickup. It is characterized by the midrange richness and tight bass response inherent to alder. Additionally, the 21-fret design with protruding frets ensures excellent sound projection during slap playing, which is another defining feature of its sound.


While based on the classic Jazz Bass format, this instrument showcases meticulous attention to detail. It delivers a rich, resonant bass sound that stands out even within a band ensemble.

Please take this opportunity to consider it.

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――


The “MJ” is part of the original series from the popular Japanese brand “Momose.”

This 5-string model combines traditional specifications with modern playability and sound.


The model featured here is the “MJ・Five1-STD/R,” a jazz bass featuring an alder body, a maple neck, and a rosewood fingerboard.


Craftsmanship is evident throughout, such as the slanted body-to-neck joint and recessed joint plate—designed to improve playability in the higher registers—and the wood binding along the fingerboard edges that conceals the fret posts.

The headstock features a five-in-line tuning peg configuration, with all five pegs arranged on one side. This larger headstock design increases neck mass, which positively affects the sound, resulting in a characteristic tone with a solid low-end presence.


It is equipped with the original VJ-5 pickup.

It features the midrange richness and stable low end characteristic of alder, along with the sharp sound typical of a jazz bass. Additionally, the 21-fret design with protruding frets ensures excellent sound projection during slap playing.

The Gotoh 404BO-5 bridge is compatible with both through-body and under-body string routing, allowing players to adjust the string tension to their preference.


Based on the classic Jazz Bass format, this instrument showcases meticulous attention to detail.

We encourage you to take this opportunity to consider it.

-----------------------------------

Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

[Slightly Damaged - Special Price]

The Schecter DIAMOND SERIES BASS enjoys immense popularity for its heavy and loud sound. The STILETTO model, with its distinctive body shape and eye-catching exotic wood grain, boasts both a great look and the excellent cost performance characteristic of the series. With its resonant sound thanks to its through-neck construction, and an easy-to-handle neck grip and body balance, this model can be used in a wide range of genres.

The body features a flame maple top and mahogany back, while the neck is a 5-piece construction of maple and walnut with a rosewood fingerboard. The neck-through design ensures that neck and body vibrations are transmitted without loss, resulting in a rich and deep sound. Equipped with EMG passive "EMG-HZ" pickups and a 3-band EQ, it allows for clear and versatile sound shaping.

Featuring a unique neck-through construction, this guitar boasts a distinctive, resonant bass and a crisp, well-defined midrange. The 3-band EQ allows for settings ranging from aggressive tones to a calm, warm sound. The tight, controlled bass delivers a robust, unyielding presence. Even though it's an EMG-HZ passive model, it's still an EMG! Low noise, clear soundstage, and powerful tone are all present!

Whether you're looking for a powerful sound or a model with a unique look, be sure to check this out!

*There may be minor scratches or imperfections that are not visible in the images, but this does not affect the instrument's performance.

■BODY: Mahogany w/Flamed Maple
■NECK: Multi-Laminate Maple & Walnut
■CONSTRUCTION: Neck-thru w/Ultra Access
■FINGERBOARD: Rosewood
■FRETS: 24 X-Jumbo
■SCALE: 34
■INLAY: Offset Dots
■PICKUPS: EMG HZ
■BRIDGE: Diamond Custom
■TUNERS: Schecter
■HARDWARE: Black Chrome
■CONTROL: Master Vol / Master Blend / EMG Active 18v 3-Band EQ

Gig bag included

item.title: Sire V7 - Natural — $425
item.description:
$425 · Very Good · Jeremiah's Gear Garage

Arguably the best Bass I've ever owned. Definitely the best value bass I've ever played in my life. In very good condition for a decade old instrument that has a hundreds of hours of play on it.

Bass has the fastest neck I've ever played. Preamp and tonal versatility are A++. Every tone in the book. Checks every block. The best slapper I've ever owned.

Also I'm this is one of the first gen models when Sire was really in the fine details on the craftsmanship, still good, but this bass is imo PEAK Sire. They were out to prove something when they made his one.

Don't want to let it go but I got 3 V7s. I just have too many basses and need to sell some away. Not right or a nice instrument to go unplayed.

$425 OBO

item.title: [Original Color]saitias guitars / Lorentz 5 CTM "All Black" [2.91kg][Made in Japan] — $2,511.54
item.description:
$2,511.54 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

-----------------------------------

*Please note that this text was translated using a translation tool.

*The color of the image may appear different depending on the monitor you are using.

Please feel free to contact us if you would like to see the image in the store.


--------------------------------------------


Based in Chigasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Saitias Guitars is a brand garnering significant attention—including an upcoming appearance at the NAMM Show 2026—driven by a philosophy of creating unique, one-of-a-kind instruments that inspire deep affection in everyone, from the players who hold them to the people involved in selling them.


We are thrilled to announce the arrival of the Lorentz 5 CTM "All Black"—a custom model ordered by our shop that sold out immediately last year!


The Lorentz 5 is a headless model featuring a 30.5-inch short scale. This particular unit boasts an exceptionally lightweight body (2.91 kg), offering outstanding playability and ease of handling.


The instrument features a well-balanced alder body and a maple neck, paired with an ebony fingerboard that delivers a hard, articulate tone. While truly jet-black ebony has become increasingly rare in recent years, this unit utilizes a specially selected piece of that very quality; its silky texture—revealing a subtle sheen when held against the light—accentuates the instrument's overall sense of luxury.


It is equipped with Nordstrand "Bigman 5" pickups. These deliver intense punch and a thick, powerful low-mid range while maintaining excellent high-end clarity and edge, ensuring that slap attacks and ghost notes are reproduced with great definition.


The preamp is the "4117" model from the Japanese brand Humpback Engineering. Designed with the Music Man sound in mind, the 4117 offers a hi-fi yet natural tonal character and highly responsive EQ controls.


In addition to a standard three-knob layout (Volume, Treble, Bass), the bass features mini-switches for 3-way pickup switching (series, parallel, single-coil) and active/passive mode selection, providing the versatility to handle a wide range of musical genres.


The headless-specific bridge from Nova Bridge not only ensures easy tuning but also offers a striking, mechanical aesthetic that perfectly complements the instrument's design. The bridge features 18mm string spacing, accommodating technical fingerstyle playing.

It sports a 21-fret configuration, and the 12-inch fretboard radius—a relatively flat curve—facilitates smooth fingering.


The black body finish is not a flat black; it possesses a rich depth with a subtle, brownish nuance.

The headstock is also black, creating a unified, cool aesthetic, while an acrylic tortoiseshell pickguard adds a chic, subtle accent.


Custom inlays combining pearl dots with brass rings lend an air of elegance and premium quality.


A "head cover" (knit cap) is included as an accessory.

According to Mr. Saita (the company representative), the cover is provided so that the instrument—which leaves cut string ends exposed due to its headless design—can be displayed safely in households with children.


Additionally, the headstock features a unique design that allows the instrument to be used with standard hanging-style stands, despite being headless.


From structural innovations to the included head cover, this model embodies Mr. Saita’s vision of creating a "one-of-a-kind product that everyone can grow to love," with meticulous attention paid to every detail.


A standout instrument that shines with solid quality and innovative ideas.

We invite you to consider this exceptional model.


We look forward to your inquiries.


~Specifications~

Body Color: Black

Body Wood: Alder

Neck: Maple

Fretboard: Ebony

Inlay: Pearl Dot w/ Brass ring

String Spacing: 18mm

Scale: 30.5"

Radius: 12"

Frets: 21

Nut: Black TUSQ

Pickguard: Acrylic Tortoiseshell

Bridge: Nova Headless Bridge (5-string)

Pickup: Nordstrand Bigman 5

Preamp: Humpback Engineering 4117

Controls: Vol/Treble/Bass/3-way PU Position Mini-Switch/

Active/Passive Mini-Switch

Hardware Color: Black

Blood Sugar Sex Magik era Flea tone, Bernard Edward's flatwound "chucking" tone from Chic. Love the blue pearl limited edition finish. item.title: Ibanez BTB605MS-SOM [4.60kg][SPOT Model] — $1,012.98
item.description:
$1,012.98 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

-----------------------------------

A new model has arrived from Ibanez’s popular BTB series, featuring multi-scale technology!

True to its color name, “Solar Eclipse Matte”—which means “solar eclipse”—it boasts a stylish finish where the color gradually lightens toward the outer edges! The matte texture adds a touch of luxury and complements the design perfectly.



[Features]



◆ Rich tone and excellent playability thanks to the through-neck construction


This model features a “through-neck” construction where the neck and body are integrated as a single unit. Since there is no joint, string vibrations are transmitted directly to the entire body without obstruction, delivering rich sustain.

Additionally, the absence of a thick neck heel allows for smooth, stress-free fingering all the way up to the 24th fret.



◆ Multi-Scale Design That Balances Playability and Punchy Bass


The multi-scale design is the hallmark of the BTB series. This model is designed with a scale length ranging from 37 inches on the 5th string to 35 inches on the 1st string.

This design maintains ease of fingering while preserving string tension during down-tuning. Furthermore, it ensures a tight, resonant tone consistent with the other strings, producing well-defined, deep bass that never gets lost in an ensemble.



◆ 5-Piece Neck Construction Balancing Rigidity and Acoustic Characteristics


It features a 5-piece neck construction that alternates layers of maple—known for its clear tone and rich attack—with hard walnut.

Combining these different woods not only creates a sturdy neck resistant to warping and twisting but also further enhances rigidity through an internal graphite rod. This maintains stable performance over the long term while producing a tight, responsive sound.



◆ Tight, Noise-Free Original “T1” Pickup


Equipped with Ibanez’s original “T1” pickup, which features two individually mounted coils to deliver tight bass response, crisp dynamics, and punchy mids.

Each coil is ergonomically positioned to maintain balanced volume across all strings—even on multi-scale models—ensuring a clear and consistent sound.



◆ 3-Band EQ for Intuitive and Versatile Sound Shaping


Equipped with Ibanez’s original 3-band EQ, which produces a wide-range, low-noise active sound.

The midrange—the cornerstone of the sound—can be instantly switched via a mini-switch between three frequency bands: 250 Hz (body and sustain), 450 Hz (clarity and presence), and 700 Hz (attack and definition). This provides powerful support for precise tone shaping tailored to your ensemble or musical genre.


Furthermore, the EQ bypass switch allows you to switch to a warm, natural passive sound. When bypassed, the Treble knob functions as a passive tone control, enabling seamless tone shaping regardless of whether the EQ is active or passive. (Powered by a 006P 9V battery)



◆ MR5S Monorail Bridge: Maximizes the Resonance of Each String


Features the “MR5S (Monorail)” bridge, which provides a completely independent structure for each string.

By eliminating unwanted vibration interference between adjacent strings, each note is clearly separated without muddiness, and the string’s natural vibration is transmitted directly to the body.


Furthermore, it features a “String Spacing Adjustment Mechanism” that allows the saddles to be adjusted ±1.5 mm left and right. This enables you to finely tune the spacing between the strings (string spacing) to suit your playing style and hand size, allowing you to pursue the optimal playability that suits your preferences.



【Spec】

neck：5pc Maple/Walnut w/Graphite reinforcement rodsNeck-through

body：Okoume wing body

fretboard：Bound Panga Panga

fret：Medium Stainless Steel frets　24 (+ zero fret)

bridge：MR5S bridge

string space：18mm

neck pickup：T1 neck pickupPassive
bridge pickup：T1 bridge pickupPassive

equalizer：Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ w/ EQ bypass switch (passive tone control on treble pot) & 3-way Mid frequency switch

string gauge：045/.055/.080/.100MS/.130MS

hardware color：Black

Scale：939.8mm/37" - 889mm/35"

a : Width 47mm at NUT
b : Width 75mm at 24F
c : Thickness 20mm at 1F
d : Thickness 22mm at 12F

Radius：400mmR


[Accessories]

Semi-hard case, hex wrench


----------------------------------

item.title: Fender Marcus Miller Artist Series Signature Jazz Bass with Sadowsky Electronics — $1,800
item.description:
$1,800 · Very Good · Masanori's Gear Depot

Stunning natural finish ash body Fender Marcus Miller Signature Jazz Bass. This four-string electric bass has a maple neck with block inlays and a matching maple fingerboard. It is equipped with a Badass Bass II bridge, a chrome pickup cover, and a black pickguard. The headstock has the Fender Jazz Bass logo and the Marcus Miller signature. 

Electronics has been completely swapped out with Sadowsky hum-cancelling Jazz Bass pickups as well as the Sadowsky On-board preamp with Vintage Tone Control.

The entire cavity has been treated with conductive paint to transform the bass completely  noise free.

A Fender hardshell tweed case with a red plush interior is included.

Killer Marcus Miller sound bass with a nice aged natural finish and full upgraded electronics.

Pickup in Southern CA area. Can be shipped, but will need to discuss shipping options.

item.description:
$1,000 · Very Good · Jeremiah's Gear Garage

This is a player's bass, thousand of hours of playing. Has a few chips aand wear commiserate with being heavily played. Nearly 20 years old. Sounding better every year. I've played this a ton. Fits in every mix perfectly. Great with rounds. Great with flats. Records with the best basses I've ever played. Has a jazz profile neck. I actually don't wanna sell this deep in my bass playing heart its a bass the tells you why Duck put down his fender and played the Lakland 44-64 to the end of his days. I myself put down an early 70s Fender for this one. Great bass. Pure classic Precision bass tone. $1000 obo

item.title: FUJIGEN(FGN) JMP2B-ASH-M -Open Pore Black- #G260083[4.37kg][Made In Japan] — $1,080.51
item.description:
$1,080.51 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆


Among the many FUJIGEN guitars in stock, this one stands out! !Just look at this look.

First of all, the bold specification of "two P-type pickups" is what immediately catches the eye.

In fact, this is a very popular model, receiving constant requests from players both domestically and internationally who say, "I've been waiting for this specification!"


Its reputation has spread in Japan, and the number of people purchasing it has increased, so now I, the person in charge, can confidently recommend this model.

And what's more, this isn't just any model; it's a [limited production item]!

Incidentally, the blue model is already sold out, and we really don't know when this model will also become unavailable...


A limited edition specification packed with playfulness and attention to detail not found in the regular line.

Before you regret not buying it then, be sure to check it out!


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


The Craftsmanship Shines Through in the "Open Pore Satin Finish"

I hope this texture comes across in the images...!


This finish utilizes the natural irregularities of the wood, leaving the wood grain unfilled.

It's characterized by the direct texture unique to ash, and the powerful grain of the ash stands out against the chic matte black...

Because this finish leaves no room for error, it embodies the skills of Fujigen's proud, highly skilled craftsmen.


The headstock, finished in the same matte black as the body, looks fantastic and really tightens up the overall look.

This is a guitar that exudes "mature charm" and is sure to look great on stage!


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


【Sound】

A surprisingly "thick and versatile" tone that belies its appearance!

With this look, you might think it has a "strong character," but it actually has an incredibly "usable" sound!


It's luxuriously equipped with two Seymour Duncan split-coil pickups.

While retaining the "softness," "thickness," and "elasticity" characteristic of the Precision Bass, it has evolved into a more powerful and dense sound.

The best way to describe it is "a Jazz Bass sound, but thicker and more robust."

Depending on the tone and volume adjustments and playing technique, you can even get a surprisingly natural Precision Bass sound.


And it pairs perfectly with distortion!


It's designed with heavy, pick-played metal and hardcore in mind.


Even when tuned down to Drop D, the sound doesn't become muddy, and the sheer power of the "wall of sound" when distortion is applied is breathtaking!


【Playability】

It features a stress-free, aggressive design.


The back of the neck has a smooth, matte (satin) finish.


It doesn't get sticky even when you sweat, and position changes are smooth!


This is an irresistible feature for players who frequently use fast playing and melodic phrases.


The nut is 40mm wide, slightly narrower than a typical Precision Bass.


The shape has a comfortable thickness, yet it fits comfortably even for those with small hands or female players.


You can grip it without putting unnecessary force on your thumb, making it less tiring even during long playing sessions.


And it's easy to reach the high positions!


The body joint is rounded, allowing for smooth access to the high frets. Furthermore, the contoured body perfectly matches the contours of your body, providing an incredible sense of unity when you hold it!


The satin finish on the body makes it easy to play, even with techniques that involve a lot of right-hand movement—a unique feature of this model.


"I like the sound of a Precision Bass, but I want a little more power."


"I want the playability of a Jazz Bass but with a thicker sound."... This model fulfills those ambitious desires.


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


【Specifications】

1. Wood Composition

Ash body, maple fingerboard, maple neck


2. Electronics

Traditional 2 volume, 1 tone

Equipped with Seymour Duncan Quarter-Pound SPB-3 pickups.


3. Hardware

Features a string-through-body bridge.

Down tuning is also considered, allowing you to choose your preferred stringing method.


4. Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.)

Fujigen's unique patented technology.

By installing the frets in an arc shape, each string and fret intersect at a right angle, achieving accurate pitch and rich sustain.

The clear sound with less muddiness in chords is a great asset in ensembles.


5. Finish

Open-pore finish uses urethane.

Many people have the image of a thick, glossy finish, but as with this model, "urethane that is deliberately applied thinly by craftsmen, preserving the wood's natural texture" is actually a very time-consuming and luxurious finish.


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿◆


【Accessories】

Soft case, hex wrench


【SPEC】

Body: Ash

Neck: Maple U-Shape

Fingerboard: Maple 21F Medium Jumbo C.F.S.

Scale: 863.6 mm / 34 in

Bridge: FGBB-104 (String Pitch = 19mm)

Tuning: GOTOH GB11W

Control: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

Pickup: Seymour Duncan Quarter Pound SPB-3

item.title: APB-101 -Olympic White- #260234[3.83kg][Made In Japan] — $1,054.14
item.description:
$1,054.14 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

----- *Important Notice!!


To prevent fraudulent purchases, PayPal guest checkout is not available.


Please create a PayPal account before payment.

*It appears possible to pay by credit card on the Reverb marketplace without using PayPal; however, this actually processes the transaction as a PayPal guest checkout, and the order is not valid.

-----

Welcome to Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We ship worldwide!!


Thank you for viewing!


Please feel free to contact us with any questions.


Also, please carefully read the shop policy located below the guitar description.


-----------------------------------------

It offers astonishing value for money in today's instrument market, sitting in the 100,000-yen price range.

This is by no means a "cheap model"; it embodies the results of Japanese craftsmen—handling everything from wood selection to assembly—genuinely pursuing the virtues of a "classic, high-quality bass."


It features a chic look that pairs a stark white body with a black pickguard!

The striking contrast between the white and black evokes the vibe of early punk or new wave—a supremely cool, edgy, and quintessential rock icon.


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆



【Sound】

Whether you play with a groovy fingerstyle approach or dig in hard with a pick, the playing experience is incredibly satisfying!

The fact that it captures a vintage vibe—reminiscent of American-made Fenders—is a highly attractive feature at this price point.


At the core of the sound are pickups from the renowned brand Gotoh.

When played with fingers, it delivers a pleasant, "sticky" sustain reminiscent of Motown, with a straight, woody bass tone that resonates deep within your soul.

If you pick aggressively, it transforms into a punchy, gritty, and commanding drive sound that leverages the solid, resonant core of the alder body.

It delivers a bold, sharp edge that cuts through the mix, making it perfect for rock or punk settings.


My recommended setting is to roll off the tone knob slightly; this yields a wonderfully sweet, deep vintage tone that is a perfect match for Motown or old-school funk.

Despite its extremely simple 1-volume, 1-tone configuration, the instrument allows for a surprisingly expressive range of tones depending on the player's touch. Whether playing backing for vocals, R&B, or pop, this bass effortlessly delivers that ideal Precision Bass low-end—gently enveloping the entire band while allowing the bassline to assert itself just the right amount.




【Playability】

Its standout feature is the incredible playability resulting from a narrower nut width!

When people think of the Precision Bass, they often imagine:

・A thick neck that is hard to grip

・An instrument that feels intimidating for players with smaller hands

...right?


This model completely shatters those preconceptions!

Designed with a nut width about 2mm slimmer than standard PB types, it offers a grip that feels amazingly smooth—almost as if you had switched over from a Jazz Bass.

It boasts playability suited for every bassist, dramatically reducing strain on the left hand whether you are playing root notes in the low positions or quickly shifting to the high frets.



The neck features a slim profile with a slightly rounded feel and a classic gloss finish.

While it intentionally sports a classic gloss finish, a key highlight is the use of a compound radius fretboard.

It features a compound radius ranging from 184mm to 260mm (approx. 7.5" to 10"), flattening out as you move up the neck.

This design balances ease of gripping in the lower frets with modern playability in the higher frets, preventing notes from choking out during string bends.



You get the rich, thick, and punchy character of an alder body paired with passive Precision Bass electronics—delivering that authentic, gritty, and cool PB vibe to perfection.

If you’ve wanted the Precision Bass sound but were put off by neck thickness or overly stiff tones, this is the ultimate instrument for you.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




【Specifications】

1. Wood Configuration

Alder body, rosewood fretboard, maple neck.

This is the classic, reliable combination that matches the standard specs of Fender USA models. 2. Bridge

It features a single-groove saddle design that prevents "string slippage" without compromising the vintage aesthetic, ensuring stability even during aggressive picking.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




【A Message from the Tokai Bass Specialist】

Having long been captivated by the appeal of basses crafted by Tokai Gakki, we at Kurosawa Music have begun carrying them to share that charm with our customers.

While they may sometimes be overshadowed by the countless brands worldwide, these instruments—handcrafted one by one with the heart and soul of skilled Japanese artisans—convey their exquisite precision and passion the moment you pick them up.

As a music store staff member, it brings me immense joy to introduce this wonderful example of "Japanese craftsmanship" to you and to share the delight of making music together.

We invite you to visit our Ikebukuro store and experience the true quality of these instruments for yourself.



【Included Accessories】

Original gig bag, wrench

*Specifications have been updated to include a premium-quality gig bag!*


*Please note that the price has increased due to a revision.*



【SPEC】

Body

Body Shape: Precision Bass Type

Wood Material: Alder (2-Piece)

Body Finish: Gloss Urethane


Neck

Wood Material: Maple

Scale Length: 863.6 mm / 34 in

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Number Of Frets: 20F

Fingerboard Radius: R184–R260 (Compound Radius)

Nut Width: 40.0 mm


Hardware

Bridge: BB-VB Bridge (String Pitch = 19 mm)

Controls: 1 Volume, 1 Tone

Pickup: PB-Vintage MK3

item.title: Sire 2nd Generation Marcus Miller V7 Alder 5-String with Ebony Fretboard 2019 - Burgundy — $625
item.description:
$625 · Excellent · Jeremiah's Gear Garage

Sire V7 is the best bang for your buck bass on the planet. Bass checks every box from playability to tonal versatility. Slaps, thumps, growls, vintage tone, modern tone. Preamp is my favorite ever. B string is solid. About as fast and balanced a fiver as you will find. Not a thing it doesn't do really well, and its gorgeous. Burgundy Mist is a spectacular finish. 650 OBO.

item.title: Fender USA American Vintage '62 Jazz Bass 3-Color Sunburst 3-Knob Export Version, 1998 [V093255] (07/17) — $2,186.74
item.description:
$2,186.74 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A 1998 JB?the Japan-exclusive 3-knob export version!

A 1998 American Vintage Series Jazz Bass has arrived.
At the time, the worldwide specification for the American Vintage '62 Jazz Bass featured a vintage JB control layout with two stacked knobs for 2 volume and 2 tone controls; however, this particular instrument is an export version designed specifically for the Japanese market, featuring a simple, basic, and standard 3-knob control layout with 2 volume and 1 master tone controls.
As for its condition, this instrument is now 28 years old.
There are some dents, scratches, paint deterioration, and signs of wear on the metal partsf plating (such as rust) and some discoloration; however, given its age, these may be considered natural signs of aging.
However, the finish retains a rich luster, and there are no signs of wear on the frets, suggesting it may not have been used for serious performance or heavy-duty playing.
Furthermore, aside from normal wear and tear like scratches, there are no signs of rough handling, so it may have been stored in a hard case for a long time.
While there are some noticeable scratches, this instrument gives off a very clean and well-maintained impression.
Furthermore, the pots have been replaced with 2023 CTS models, and the output jack has been upgraded to a Switchcraft #11 mono jack.
The workmanship is beautiful, suggesting this was a professional repair carried out in recent years.
Furthermore, the pickguard mounting screws, which had suffered significant rust damage, have been replaced with genuine Fender USA screws.
All other parts are in their original condition.
It also comes with some accessories?now considered rare?in unused condition, still attached to the guitar.
For further details, please contact the guitar sales staff at our Nagoya Sakae store!

Condition Grade: B+: Some minor scratches, but no issues for normal use

Serial Number: V 093255
Year of Manufacture: 1998
Country of Manufacture: United States
Case: Hard Case
Accessories: English quick start guide, English spec sheet, various tags, pickup fence, bridge cover, 60s Jazz Bass mute system kit (not installed)
Weight: 4.09 kg
Truss Rod: No issues; checked up to 60 degrees to the left and right with no problems
Fret Wear: No issues; in like-new condition
String Height (Low String Side): 3.0 mm at the 12th fret
String height (low-string side): 2.5 mm at the 12th fret
Nut width: 38.0 mm
Neck thickness near the 1st fret: 21.0 mm
Neck thickness near the 12th fret: 24.9 mm
Scale: 864 mm (34")
RADIUS: 184 mm (7.25")
Strings at setup: D'Addario EXL165 / .045-.105 set
Modifications: Pickguard mounting screws replaced (Fender P.N. #0994923000)
Repairs and Maintenance: Cleaning, string replacement, truss rod adjustment, octave adjustment, action adjustment

item.title: Rivolta Guitars Combinata Bass VII 2019 - Present - Autunno Burst — $950
item.description:
$950 · Excellent · Jeremiah's Gear Garage

Designed by Dennus Fano a legend of retro-mod aesthitic instruments. 32 scale, slim taper set neck with a medium 'C' profile, chambered mahogany body and German carve top.

Cool factor off the charts on this bass. Pickups remind me alot of rickenbacker and a hint of stingray to it.

Goes between classic growl and warm smooth thump. Slaps surprisingly well for something I think aims at a Rick. Takes a pick with the best of them. Records like a champ. High on my list of best sounding basses in a mix that I've played.

This is an instrument that will turn heads and get compliments on both looks and tone. Hard to let it go cuz i actually got it during a time they were hard to find took me a year to find one that wasn't obscenely overpriced. New from Rivolta is currently 1300. I'll take 950 OBO.

item.title: FENDER PRECISION BASS AMERICAN PRO II MN BLK — $2,550.24
item.description:
$2,550.24 · Brand New · MUSIC IN

The American Professional II Precision Bass® draws from more than sixty years of innovation, inspiration and evolution to meet the demands of today's working player. The '63 P Bass profile neck now sports smooth rolled fingerboard edges, a "Super-Natural" satin finish and a newly sculpted neck heel for a supremely comfortable feel and easy access to the upper register. The new V-Mod II Precision Bass split-coil pickup is more articulate than ever while delivering the punch and growl the P Bass® is known for. The American Pro II Precision Bass delivers instant familiarity and sonic versatility you'll feel and hear right away, with broad ranging improvements that add up to nothing less than a new standard for professional instruments. Features Single V-Mod II split-coil Precision Bass pickup '63 P Bass neck profile Bone nut; 20 narrow-tall frets for familiar playing feel HiMass™ Vintage bridge for increased sustain Tapered-shaft tuning machines; Posiflex™ graphite rods for neck reinforcement Includes Deluxe Molded hardshell case

item.description:
$1,234.87 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A high-performance bass made in the USA with a satin lacquer finish!
The Fender Highway One Precision Bass features an ultra-thin nitrocellulose lacquer finish that delivers a natural tone and beautiful patina over time. It features an alder body, a maple neck (Modern C profile), a rosewood fingerboard with a 9.5-inch radius, and medium-jumbo frets.
It is equipped with a vintage-style Alnico split single-coil Precision Bass pickup and a Greasebucket tone circuit designed to roll off the highs without muddying the lows. The BADASS II bridge ensures excellent sustain and stability. This highly practical model successfully balances vintage character with modern playability.
There are dents, paint scuffs, and scratches on the elbow contour area.
There is also a shallow paint chip on the side of the body.

item.title: Guild Starfire I Bass 2023 - Present - Pelham Blue — $500
item.description:
$500 · Excellent · Jeremiah's Gear Garage

Great thumpy Guild Starfire full of classic bass tones. Shortie with thick tone, strung with flats and plays easy. Extremely fun bass in an absolutely gorgeous blue finish. It served admirably as Pbass when I had to get a refret on my number one and go without for a stretch. 30 scale. Hollow body. Surprisingly versatile but really does the chunky thumpy vintage thing to the Nth degree. Id love to hear it when the flats age into peak form.

500 OBO. Thanks for your interest.

item.title: Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass Fretless 2020 - Present - Dark Night — $1,192
item.description:
$1,192 · Excellent · Sky Designs

This is an excellent condition fretless Jazz bass looking for a good home. It has barely been played and never gigged. It has spent most of its life in the included har case in a climate controlled, smoke free environment.

item.title: Fender Player Jazz Bass 2022 - Tidepool Blue — $785
item.description:
$785 · Excellent · AV Music

Fender Player Jazz Bass

  • Tidepool Blue Finish alder body
  • Maple Neck
  • 4 string; currently using Aurora blue strings
  • Fender HiMass Bridge - upgrade from the factory standard stamped bridge and improves sustain and tone
  • HipShot Bass Xtender - set to drop from E to D with a quick flip of a switch
  • Schaller Strap lock buttons
  • Includes a Fender woven strap with Schaller strap locks
  • Alnico 5 single coil pickups
  • C shape neck
  • Fender aged white pickguard

This Jazz Bass is in excellent condition. It sounds and plays great. The HiMass bridge provides more sustain and better tone than the factory stamped bridge. The HipShot Xtender lets you effortlessly drop to D without losing tuning (can be adjusted based on string size also). The finish is in excellent condition. Everything plays and functions as new.

item.title: Ibanez SR1006EFM-NTF Prestige 2006 Aguilar OBP-3 preamp — $1,300
item.description:
$1,300 · Very Good · Peezy’s Playhouse

This 2006 Ibanez SR1006 prestige model comes upgraded with an Aguilar OBP-3 preamp as well as a hardshell case. This bass comes in at around 10.5 lbs and has great balance between the mono rail bridge and the gotoh tuners. The neck finish has also been sanded off the back for an incredibly fast and easy playing experience. You can see the difference in shade of the removed finish where the body and neck meet. One of the original knobs was also replaced with a slightly smaller one from another Ibanez bass because of cracking. Dunlop strap locks have been added as well for extra security. One of the back covers for the tuners had fallen off and exposes the gears, but beyond that there are very few scratches and dings on this beauty.

item.title: APB-101 -Gun Metallic Blue- #260230[3.87kg][Made In Japan] — $1,054.14
item.description:
$1,054.14 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

----- *Important Notice!!


To prevent fraudulent purchases, PayPal guest checkout is not available.


Please create a PayPal account before payment.

*It appears possible to pay by credit card on the Reverb marketplace without using PayPal; however, this actually processes the transaction as a PayPal guest checkout, and the order is not valid.

-----

Welcome to Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We ship worldwide!!


Thank you for viewing!


Please feel free to contact us with any questions.


Also, please carefully read the shop policy located below the guitar description.


-----------------------------------------

It offers astonishing value for money in today's instrument market, sitting in the 100,000-yen price range.

This is by no means a "cheap model"; it embodies the results of Japanese craftsmen—handling everything from wood selection to assembly—genuinely pursuing the virtues of a "classic, high-quality bass."


It boasts a cool aesthetic, pairing a dazzling metallic blue finish with a black pickguard!

It isn't just any blue; it is a profound metallic blue where dark, metallic hues harmonize exquisitely.

Under dim lighting, it appears as a mysterious deep blue, but when stage lights hit it, the fine metallic particles gleam brilliantly, transforming its look into something sharp and dynamic.


◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆



【Sound】

Whether you play with a groovy fingerstyle approach or dig in hard with a pick, the playing experience is incredibly satisfying!

The fact that it captures a vintage vibe—reminiscent of American-made Fenders—is a highly attractive feature at this price point.


At the core of the sound are pickups from the renowned brand Gotoh.

When played with fingers, it delivers a pleasant, "sticky" sustain reminiscent of Motown, with a straight, woody bass tone that resonates deep within your soul.

If you pick aggressively, it transforms into a punchy, gritty, and commanding drive sound that leverages the solid, resonant core of the alder body.

It delivers a bold, sharp edge that cuts through the mix, making it perfect for rock or punk settings.


My recommended setting is to roll off the tone knob slightly; this yields a wonderfully sweet, deep vintage tone that is a perfect match for Motown or old-school funk.

Despite its extremely simple 1-volume, 1-tone configuration, the instrument allows for a surprisingly expressive range of tones depending on the player's touch. Whether playing backing for vocals, R&B, or pop, this bass effortlessly delivers that ideal Precision Bass low-end—gently enveloping the entire band while allowing the bassline to assert itself just the right amount.




【Playability】

Its standout feature is the incredible playability resulting from a narrower nut width!

When people think of the Precision Bass, they often imagine:

・A thick neck that is hard to grip

・An instrument that feels intimidating for players with smaller hands

...right?


This model completely shatters those preconceptions!

Designed with a nut width about 2mm slimmer than standard PB types, it offers a grip that feels amazingly smooth—almost as if you had switched over from a Jazz Bass.

It boasts playability suited for every bassist, dramatically reducing strain on the left hand whether you are playing root notes in the low positions or quickly shifting to the high frets.



The neck features a slim profile with a slightly rounded feel and a classic gloss finish.

While it intentionally sports a classic gloss finish, a key highlight is the use of a compound radius fretboard.

It features a compound radius ranging from 184mm to 260mm (approx. 7.5" to 10"), flattening out as you move up the neck.

This design balances ease of gripping in the lower frets with modern playability in the higher frets, preventing notes from choking out during string bends.



You get the rich, thick, and punchy character of an alder body paired with passive Precision Bass electronics—delivering that authentic, gritty, and cool PB vibe to perfection.

If you’ve wanted the Precision Bass sound but were put off by neck thickness or overly stiff tones, this is the ultimate instrument for you.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




【Specifications】

1. Wood Configuration

Alder body, rosewood fretboard, maple neck.

This is the classic, reliable combination that matches the standard specs of Fender USA models. 2. Bridge

It features a single-groove saddle design that prevents "string slippage" without compromising the vintage aesthetic, ensuring stability even during aggressive picking.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆




【A Message from the Tokai Bass Specialist】

Having long been captivated by the appeal of basses crafted by Tokai Gakki, we at Kurosawa Music have begun carrying them to share that charm with our customers.

While they may sometimes be overshadowed by the countless brands worldwide, these instruments—handcrafted one by one with the heart and soul of skilled Japanese artisans—convey their exquisite precision and passion the moment you pick them up.

As a music store staff member, it brings me immense joy to introduce this wonderful example of "Japanese craftsmanship" to you and to share the delight of making music together.

We invite you to visit our Ikebukuro store and experience the true quality of these instruments for yourself.



【Included Accessories】

Original gig bag, wrench

*Specifications have been updated to include a premium-quality gig bag!*


*Please note that the price has increased due to a revision.*



【SPEC】

Body

Body Shape: Precision Bass Type

Wood Material: Alder (2-Piece)

Body Finish: Gloss Urethane


Neck

Wood Material: Maple

Scale Length: 863.6 mm / 34 in

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Number Of Frets: 20F

Fingerboard Radius: R184–R260 (Compound Radius)

Nut Width: 40.0 mm


Hardware

Bridge: BB-VB Bridge (String Pitch = 19 mm)

Controls: 1 Volume, 1 Tone

Pickup: PB-Vintage MK3


*The product listed here is also sold in-store and on other websites. While we strive to update stock information promptly, please accept our apologies and understanding should the item be sold out at the time of your order.

This item is sold both online and in our physical store, so it may be sold out. In that case, your order will be canceled. We do our best to keep our inventory updated, but please understand that such situations may be unavoidable due to the operation of our physical store.

Customs Duties

item.title: 1983 Aria Pro II Japan WL Series "Wedge" Bass (Metallic Red) — $1,128.20
item.description:
$1,128.20 · Good · Cool Old Guitars

A cult classic in the world of Japanese guitars, the WL "Wedge" bass was made by Aria Pro II in the mid 1980's during their experimental era or weird and wacky designs. Solid alder body in a sleek, futuristic shape bolted to a maple neck with a 24 fret rosewood fingerboard (40mm nut width, 820mm medium scale length), it weighs 3.7kg's. 
Looks to be all original and working well, it's in good used condition with only basic signs of wear from general use. Received a professional setup and service prior to listing, the neck is straight and the truss works as it should. The frets are shiny and smooth with plenty of life.
